title: aneilema lanceolatum subsp. lanceolatum
intro: Aneilema lanceolatum subsp. lanceolatum, commonly known as the lance-leaved Aneilema, is a plant species belonging to the family Commelinaceae. It is a perennial herb found in tropical regions of Africa, particularly in the savannas and grasslands. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, with its roots and leaves used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The lance-leaved Aneilema has been used in traditional medicine to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system, with herbal preparations often including infusions, decoctions, and poultices.
